caption:
Stop using Sass just for nesting.

CSS can nest natively now. No preprocessor. No build step. No dependencies.

Every major browser supports it since 2023.

Which way do you write CSS? A or B?

#css #webdev #frontend #coding #programming #developer #codingtips #sass #webdesign #html

slides.title: Which nesting is better?
slides.problem: Nest child selectors without a preprocessor

slides.title: B wins. They're identical.

verdicts.title: Requires Sass + build step
verdicts.text: You need node-sass or dart-sass, a build pipeline, a config file, and a watcher. All just to nest selectors.

verdicts.title: Native CSS — zero dependencies
verdicts.text: Same syntax works natively in the browser. No build step. No npm install. No config. Just write CSS.

slides.takeaway: The #1 reason people used Sass is now built into CSS.

slides.title: Native nesting is even better.
slides.sub: It supports things Sass can't do natively.

items.text: Works in Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge
Zero build step — just ship .css files

slides.teaser: Next: The CSS property that replaces 10 media queries
